title: The Nature of Dick's Fantasies (Review)
author: Gregg Rickman
card_summary: Review essay by Gregg Rickman in Science Fiction Studies (Vol. 19, No. 1, March 1992). Reviews Lawrence Sutin's "In Pursuit of Valis: Selections from the Exegesis" and "The Selected Letters of Philip K. Dick." Discusses the nature of Dick's visionary and fantastical experiences as documented in the Exegesis and correspondence.
page_summary: Review essay by Gregg Rickman in Science Fiction Studies (Vol. 19, No. 1, March 1992) reviewing two landmark posthumous publications: Lawrence Sutin's "In Pursuit of Valis: Selections from the Exegesis" (Underwood-Miller, 1991) and Paul Williams's "The Selected Letters of Philip K. Dick 1974" (Underwood-Miller, 1991). Rickman, who had conducted extensive interviews with Dick before his death, evaluates these primary source publications as essential documents for understanding Dick's visionary experiences of 1974 and their aftermath. The review discusses the nature of Dick's fantasies as documented in the Exegesis and his correspondence, examining the literary, political, psychological, and philosophical dimensions of Dick's post-2-3-74 writings.
